title: Mother of Learning
author: Nobody103
synopsis: Zorian is a teenage mage of humble skill and slightly above-average power. He gets trapped in a time loop, doomed to repeat the same month over and over before a massive invasion. The novel is about him going through it, learning more about the world and the invasion, and trying to find a way to stop it.
review: The best single bit of fiction I have ever read.

title: The Perfect Run
author: Maxime J. Durand
synopsis: Ryan 'Quicksave' Romano can save and load time. He arrives in a chaotic New Rome looking for an old friend, intending to find the 'Perfect Run' where everyone survives.
review: A very solid time loop story, with a superhero twist.

title: He Who Fights With Monsters
author: Shirtaloon
synopsis: Jason wakes up in a world of magic and monsters. He gains a system and a very unique set of dark powers. Now he has to navigate inter-dimensional politics and ancient cults.
review: A fun progression fantasy story that sometimes just goes on overly long side plots.

title: The Stormlight Archive
author: Brandon Sanderson
synopsis: An epic tale following a slave fighting in a senseless war, a highprince losing his mind, and a scholar trying to save her family. Set in a world where everything has a spirit.
review: Very unique power system and setting. The world-building is as excellent as you would expect from Sanderson. The story can be a bit slow at times, but the characters are interesting and the plot is engaging.

title: Iron Prince
author: Bryce O'Connor
synopsis: Rei has a genetic condition that makes his bones brittle, but he is accepted into a combat academy where students are assigned AI-controlled combat suits that evolve.
review: Zero-to-hero trope done really well. Sci-fi setting is also quite well-crafted.

title: Bastion
author: Phil Tucker
synopsis: Scoria is born in Hell, a hero that is instead branded a failure and exiled. He must survive in a dangerous city with infernal powers.
review: Very unique setting. The world building is done well, and the power system is quite creative.

title: Lord of the Mysteries
author: Cuttlefish That Loves Diving
synopsis: Klein Moretti wakes up in a Victorian-era steampunk world with beyonder powers and secret societies. He must unravel the mystery of his own death and the nature of the gods.
review: Very solid world-building. The magic system is complex and a unique eldritch vibe.

title: Beware of Chicken
author: CasualFarmer
synopsis: A man is reincarnated into a high-stakes cultivation world and decides... no thanks. He moves to a remote farm to live a peaceful life, accidentally ascending his farm animals.
review: A pretty nice slice of life stories out there. Writing is impeccable. Very large cast of characters that are well-developed and interesting.

title: A Nerubian's Journey
author: Fizzicks
synopsis: Join an unfortunate nerubian on his journey as he tries to live in a world that would very much prefer him to die. World of Warcraft isekai fanfic, some progression elements but not really the main focus.
review: I don't usually read fanfics, but this is actually just stellar writing, with a fleshed out plot, reasonable characters, and a fairly rational main character. No part of the story felt slow or rushed, but it moves faster than a lot of the 1000 chapter extended novels that go nowhere. A completed story with solid direction as well.

title: Myriad Rivers to the Sea
author: Waspak.Writer
synopsis: In a world where the strength of one's awakened spirit determines your starting point, the orphaned Li Yu awakens a low grade spirit: a blood-red Koi fish, deemed a Grade One, Inferior spirit fit only for a decorative pond
review: My greatest guilty pleasure. Straight cultivation slop of the lowest quality. Pretty sure the prose is AI generated, and the plot is quite literally too unimaginative to even be AI generated. The main character is a Mary Sue with no flaws, and the story is just a series of him getting stronger with no real conflict or stakes. The world-building is non-existent, and the writing is so bad it's almost unreadable.

title: Vainqueur the Dragon
author: Maxime J. Durand
synopsis: Vainqueur Knightsbane is your average dragon: a giant, fire-breathing lizard who loves to take naps on his golden hoard, kidnap princesses for fun, and make the life of adventurers miserable. Vainqueur's only pleasure in life is to watch his treasure get bigger, one coin at a time. So when a would-be thief turned unwilling minion tells him about classes, levels, and quests Vainqueur wonders if maybe, just maybe, he should consider a career change. After all, why bother hunting monsters for free when you can get paid for it?
review: A fun and fairly lighthearted story that ends up being more of a slice of life than an actual adventure story. The world-building is pretty solid, and the characters are interesting. The writing is decent, but it can be a bit slow at times, which just goes with the genre.

title: Reincarnated in a Shounen Manga
author: CasualBear
synopsis: Power of friendship? Getting stronger when you’re enraged? Powerful monster sealed in you by your father? Yeah, that was not me. I was the arrogant youngster who got punched on the first panels of the shounen manga I was reading. I wouldn’t have even remembered his name if I wasn’t stuck in his body.
review: Very tropey isekai story. The plot is somewhat original, but the characters themselves are very one-dimensional, and the world-building is pretty bad. The overall writing quality is fairly solid though.

title: My Best Friend is an Eldritch Horror
author: Actus
synopsis: Good things come to those who wait. Damien Vale didn't, and now he's bound to an Eldritch creature from beyond the farthest reaches of the universe. It's unfathomable, utterly evil, and likes to be called Henry. To Damien, none of that mattered. He was going to learn magic, and he'd be damned if he let something as small as this set him back.
review: A fun story about a fairly stubborn protagonist in a magic academy with a unique powerset. The world-building is pretty solid, with nontrivial plot differences from the standard formula.

title: Path To Transcendence
author: I-Ryn-I
synopsis: A second chance. An opportunity to delve down the Path to Transcendence, where Tiers and Skills reign supreme. Grief took Julian’s family. Fate gave him another life. Reborn as Julius Von Hyperius, he awakens in a world where power is measured in tiers, skills, and the will to keep climbing. Surrounded by ruthless nobles, terrifying monsters, and an unknown system, Julius has only two choices: transcend or die.  With a relentless spirit and questionable decisions, he carves his own path. Each breakthrough brings new foes, higher stakes, and the terrifying truth that transcendence demands sacrifice.
review: A pretty generic litrpg story with negligible plot or world-building, one-dimensional characters, and a pretty retarded main character.

title: Arcane Ascension
author: Andrew Rowe
synopsis: Five years ago, Corin Cadence’s brother entered the Serpent Spire — a colossal tower with ever-shifting rooms, traps, and monsters. Those who survive the spire’s trials return home with an attunement: a mark granting the bearer magical powers. According to legend, those few who reach the top of the tower will be granted a boon by the spire’s goddess. He never returned. Now, it’s Corin’s turn. He’s headed to the top floor, on a mission to meet the goddess. If he can survive the trials, Corin will earn an attunement, but that won’t be sufficient to survive the dangers on the upper levels. For that, he’s going to need training, allies, and a lot of ingenuity.
review: The series as a whole is very solid, and probably one of my favorite urban litrpg stories. That being said, the plot does get kind of repetitive from a series perspective, especially in the later books. Every book has this formulaic approach of set-up, power-leveling, random side-quest, then a boss fight at the end. Additionally the power system seems to be plot armored away whenever a relevant character is involved. THe writing itself, as well as the world-building, is very solid though. Part of Rowe's larger universe.

title: The Last Dainv
author: Majon
synopsis: Gale didn't ask to become a monster. When an anomaly opens a rift in his orphanage bedroom, Gale is thrown into the Eclipsed, where survival means mastering the alien Dainv OS or dying alone. He survives. Barely. Until he finds others. But every encounter forces an impossible choice: run and live in isolation, or fight alongside people who see him as the eldritch nightmare he's terrified of becoming. As Gale's heritage awakens, the line between human and horror blurs. To heal, he must first learn to trust—before his companions decide he's too dangerous to keep alive. The Last Dainv is a slow-burn progression LitRPG with character-driven drama, survival stakes, and a powerful MC who grows even stronger.
review: If this was a series, it would be one of those with a good first book and noone knows the rest exist. The beginning is fine for the kind of book it's trying to be, although the writing can be awkward at times. It falls off later with too many side plots, and everything to do with Mia as a character just made me dislike it even more.

title: Between Two Fires
author: Christopher Buehlman
synopsis: The year is 1348. Thomas, a disgraced knight, has found an orphan of the Black Death in a Norman village. An almost unnerving picture of innocence, she tells Thomas that the plague is only part of a larger cataclysm—that the fallen angels under Lucifer are rising in a second war on Heaven. But is it delirium or is it faith? She believes she has seen the angels of God. She believes the dead speak to her in dreams. And now she has convinced the faithless Thomas to shepherd her across an apocalyptic landscape to Avignon. There, she tells Thomas, she will fulfill her mission. There her true nature will be revealed. And there Thomas will confront an evil wrestling for the throne of Heaven, and which has poisoned his own soul.
review: A pretty fun read. Obviously a lot better written than the average webnovel slop, it's just a solid read overall. Pacing is somewhat on the slower side.

title: Sky Pride
author: Warby Picus
synopsis: The very heavens had decreed his death. He should have died in the womb. Or in infancy. Or when his family was murdered. Or when the house burned around him. Or when he woke up in the dump suffering from innumerable diseases and missing fingers. Even the magic ring with a ghostly grandpa trapped inside of it was sabotaged. A reasonable person would roll over and die. Tian isn't that reasonable. And as it happens, neither is Grandpa Jun. In a world where immortality can be cultivated, miracles are things you make for yourself. A broken boy will defy the heavens, and soar.
review: Better written than average cultivation slop, but like always the plot isn't anything special, and it does fall into the same pitfalls as others in it's genre.

title: Mythical Contract
author: Shirime
synopsis: Jujutsu Kaisen meets OG Naruto in this fun, action-packed LitRPG! Rui Payne must get stronger if he wants to remove the Fallen Star Curse from his body! In a world where humans and mythical creatures can form Contacts for mutual benefit, there are those who would attempt to make their own Myths for personal gain. These manmade creatures are called Curses, and they are the unpredictable, dangerous versions of the beings they were made to replicate. Now that he's escaped from the Cult of the Fallen Star, Rui Payne has only one goal: Remove the Curse from his body. Unfortunately for him, that's impossible! At least, that's what he thought until a strange man approached him with a proposition...
review: Lowkey the same thing as JJK and Naruto. The plot is a copy, the characters are copied, and there's nothing remotely unique about it. That being said, it is well-written, but the pacing is overly fast at times.

title: Are You Even Human
author: Thundamoo
synopsis: In 2025, the moon hatched and its child died. Things have since gotten worse. Some people have super powers now, but so do the extradimensional invaders slowly wiping humanity out. By Julietta's eighteenth birthday, the war has been loss after loss for far longer than she's been alive, so she can't bring herself to be terribly shocked when the sky splits in half and weeps death. Surviving is the real surprise. Unexpectedly gaining the powers of a shapeshifter, Julietta can change into any living thing she has touched, mixing and matching parts with flesh as mutable as flowing water. ...Except when she tries to return to her original form. Trapped behind enemy lines in someone else's skin, Julietta must adapt to new powers, new bodies, and new threats all just to survive. But when she makes it out, will she still be Julietta?
review: All this author writes is post-apocalyptic cosmic horror lesbian litrpg, and they're actually quite good at this ridiculously specific niche. Pretty solid webnovel overall, with no glaring flaws, with well fleshed-out characters and development.

title: Nova: The Final Reincarnation
author: GoldSphere
synopsis: Many lifetimes ago, Nova was a simple mafia don. Not the kind you often see in movies, but the kind that had no other options. He fought for the young and innocent to have decent lives, and then one day, he was at the top, not quite sure how he got there. As a retired mobster, he started a cat shelter. That’s the kind of guy he was. And since then, he has lived for eight lifetimes. Each one in a different world, and each time sticking to those same values. At the end of his eighth life, as the first archmage to live, he asked for a peaceful world next. But instead, he stands before his greatest challenge yet. And like always, he would either bend this new world to his will, or he would die trying.
review: Generic plot, OP Main Character, nothing special about the plot. Just some more standard (but not necessarily bad) litrpg slop.

title: Jackal Among Snakes
author: Nemorosus
synopsis: The royal bastard of House Vasquer, Argrave, changed after his stay studying magic at the Tower of the Gray Owl. The sickly man's awkward posture and cruel behavior have been replaced with a neat dignity and acerbic wit. Though once listless, he now strives towards his goals with a life-or-death tenacity. Most come away thinking he seems to know too many details about too much. Few can claim to know his motives or his goals. Yet behind that confident appearance, the primary contributor for an open-world RPG's wiki battles existential dread and things far beyond his ken as he struggles to adapt to a grim, gothic fantasy world mirroring his favorite game. The prospect of fighting against a world-ending calamity loses all of its appeal when it transitions from entertainment to reality. Argrave is spurred towards unwanted heroism to preserve his life as he struggles desperately to keep this sinking ship afloat.
review: A well-written isekai, although the pacing is less solid in the last third of the series. The main character is prone to solving issues through methods other than plain raw force, and actually utilizes his advantages well. The characters are well-developed throughout the story.
